title: Phyllorkis andersonii
taxonomy: Phyllorkis andersonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It is a member of the subtribe Coelogyneinae, which comprises several genera of orchids. The genus Phyllorkis is a relatively small group of plants and Phyllorkis andersonii is one of the species within it. Phyllorkis andersonii is native to the Himalayas and is often found growing in the wild. This plant is also known for its unique flowers and is a popular choice among orchid enthusiasts.

The Himalayan orchid has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties.
